changes: Step 3: added pre-processing requirement for whole-page/multi-component inputs — enumerate all component types present before doing the Atomica11y lookup.

Steps table replaced with a checklist (loses 'References used' column; reference files are named in each step's prose).
Step 0: dropped 'Ask them together, not one at a time' operational nag.
Step 3b.0: dropped justification sentence about silent degradation.
Step 3b.1: dropped 'Important notes' block (axe coverage gap, wcag22aa default-off, best-practice rules).
Step 3b.2: 'Important caveats' block removed from the step body; jsdom and snippet-context caveats moved into Step 4's automated test coverage output so they appear in verdicts.

Cut 'Conformance Levels' section (WCAG 101 + regulatory context; description says skip primers).

Breaking: Step 5 production logic (AC writing, usage docs) removed. Moved to new a11y-authoring skill.
Step 5 collapsed to a routing prompt that offers handoff to a11y-authoring.

Step 3b split into three sub-steps: 3b.0 environment check (Node + axe-core + jsdom) with hard-stop on missing dependencies; 3b.1 static cross-reference (previous Step 3b behaviour, unchanged); 3b.2 executable axe-core run via jsdom against supplied code, with violations / incomplete / passes output.
Step 4 'Automated test coverage' section reports which 3b path ran.
Description rewritten: drops AC and usage doc triggers, names a11y-authoring as the routing target.
